title: Unify the wordpress/jira/s3 credential-connector route files into one factory
description: Three near-identical 'simple credential connector' modules: identical resolveScope() (wordpress.ts:26-30, jira.ts:13-17, s3.ts:8-12), identical get<Provider>CredentialsForAgent agent-then-board fallback (wordpress.ts:49-62, jira.ts:29-40, s3.ts:24-34), and three structurally identical routers: GET /status (wordpress.ts:67-83, jira.ts:45-61, s3.ts:39-55), POST /connect that probes the credentials then storeOAuthToken with throwOnPersistError + the same `target = agentId ? agent "…" : board "…"` log line (wordpress.ts:85-131, jira.ts:63-103, s3.ts:57-94), and a byte-identical POST /disconnect (wordpress.ts:133-144, jira.ts:105-116, s3.ts:96-107). Only the probe call, meta shape, and status fields differ. Any fix (e.g. the timeout handling that wordpress has at :126-128 but jira/s3 lack) must currently be made three times.
impact: ~377 lines across 3 files → ~190 (factory + 3 thin configs); the agent→board scope-resolution policy and the connect/store/verify flow exist exactly once; timeout/error handling becomes uniform
guidance: Behavior-preservation traps the sketch must absorb: (1) The connect catch paths diverge and the sketch's impact line "timeout/error handling becomes uniform" is a behavior change as written — today wp maps TimeoutError→504 else 500 (wordpress.ts:124-129), jira always 500 (jira.ts:99-102) even though it HAS the same 15s AbortSignal at jira.ts:76 (it lacks only the 504 mapping), and s3 returns 400 (s3.ts:90-93). Error message prefixes also differ ('Connection failed:' vs 'S3 connection failed:'). Give the factory a per-provider onError(err)=>{status,message} knob to preserve these exactly; unify only with owner sign-off as a separate, declared wire change. (2) storeOAuthToken({throwOnPersistError:true}) throws land in that same catch, so store-failure status also differs today (wp/jira 500, s3 400) — same knob covers it. (3) wp destructures `req.body || {}` (wordpress.ts:86) while jira/s3 destructure `req.body` bare (jira.ts:64, s3.ts:58); a body-less request currently 500s on jira/s3 but 400s on wp — using `|| {}` in the factory flips jira/s3 to 400 on that degenerate input (fine, but note it). (4) The sketch omits two needed config knobs: the log label ('[WordPress]' is not capitalize('wordpress')) and the per-provider success-log suffix (`→ ${cleanUrl} (${me.name || username})` vs `→ ${cleanDomain} (${myself.displayName || email})` vs `→ ${awsRegion} (${bucketCount} buckets)`) — have connect() return a logSuffix alongside accessToken/meta/extra. (5) Per-provider required-field validation and its exact error strings belong inside connect() returning {error, status:400}, as sketched. While in there, delete the confirmed-dead has* exports (wordpress.ts:39-47, jira.ts:19-27, s3.ts:14-22) and the unused resolveAccessToken import (jira.ts:3) — no consumers exist anywhere in the repo. Since zero tests cover these routes, consider one supertest file over the factory as a safety net; the refactor itself is otherwise unguarded.

title: Extract shared task execution-state helpers in tasks.ts (stop-executor, clear-state, mem-task lookup)
description: tasks.ts repeats three micro-rituals everywhere. (1) 'stop the executing agent and clear actionRunning flags' appears verbatim in PUT /:id (tasks.ts:216-226) and bulk-move (tasks.ts:424-430), and in spirit in agents.ts PATCH (agents.ts:467-471). (2) 'clear execution state after a status change' (startedAt=null, executionStatus=null, delete _pendingOnEnter/_completedActionIdx, completedActionIdx=null, actionRunning=false, delete actionRunningAgentId/actionRunningMode, completedAt if done, setTaskSignal) appears in PUT /:id twice — once on the copy at tasks.ts:291-298 and once on the memTask at tasks.ts:345-357 — again in bulk-move at tasks.ts:458-468, and partially in /:id/stop at tasks.ts:513-524. The lists drift: the copy version at :291-298 omits _pendingOnEnter/_completedActionIdx that the memTask version clears, and a reader cannot tell if that is intentional. (3) the mem-task lookup `mgr._getAgentTasks(agentId)?.find(t => t.id === id)` appears 7 times (tasks.ts:185, 327, 453, 511, 551, 563; agents.ts:463).
impact: ~60 lines removed from tasks.ts; the 8-field 'reset a task' invariant is defined once instead of 5 drifting copies; future fields (like completedActionIdx) cannot be forgotten in one variant
guidance: Implement narrowed, not as sketched. (1) getMemTask(mgr, agentId, taskId): safe everywhere; _getAgentTasks returns [] never undefined (api/src/services/agentManager/index.ts:478-480); apply to tasks.ts:185, 327, 453, 511, 551, 563 and agents.ts:463, 511, 514, 516, 526, 546, 613. (2) stopTaskExecutor + clearActionRunning: extract for tasks.ts:216-226 and 424-430 only; helper must use `delete` (not `= null`); keep the differing outer conditions at call sites. Do NOT apply to agents.ts:467-471 (different ritual: stops req.params.id directly, never clears actionRunning flags, calls setTaskSignal) or to /:id/stop tasks.ts:513-515 (uses `= null`; delete-vs-null changes the task:updated socket payload emitted via the {...target} spread at tasks.ts:527 — saveTaskToDb is insensitive per database/tasks.ts:179-180, but the wire payload is not). (3) clearExecutionState: restrict to the 8 clear-fields (startedAt=null, executionStatus=null, delete _pendingOnEnter, delete _completedActionIdx, completedActionIdx=null, actionRunning trio) and apply ONLY at the two memTask sites tasks.ts:345-353 and 458-465, which are identical. Do NOT apply the superset to the PUT /:id copy (tasks.ts:291-298): getTask returns a shallow copy (agentManager/tasks.ts:1355-1358) persisted via saveTaskDirectly at tasks.ts:361, and completedActionIdx/_pendingOnEnter ARE persisted columns (database/tasks.ts:177 completed_action_idx, :185 pending_on_enter) — the superset would null them in the DB row where today they are retained (observable after restart rehydration). Keep the copy's shorter clear inline with a comment naming the asymmetry. (4) Keep completedAt OUT of the helper: bulk-move's memTask block never stamps completedAt even when targetColumn==='done' (tasks.ts:453-466); PUT /:id does (:297, :354) — the sketch's done-check would add a new write at bulk-move. (5) Keep setTaskSignal OUT of the helper: PUT /:id signals only inside if(memTask) (:356); bulk-move signals unconditionally outside the guard (:468, fires even with no memTask). (6) Do not reorder bulk-move: saveTaskDirectly at :449 intentionally(?) precedes the memTask clear at :452-466 — leave persistence ordering and the un-cleared copy as-is. (7) /:id/stop becomes zero calls, not one; the 'each become one call per site' claim and the agents.ts file listing in the proposal overreach. Expected savings ~35-40 lines, not ~60.

title: Replace 13 hand-rolled voice tool constants in realtime.ts with a tool() builder
description: realtime.ts:5-170 is 165 lines of 13 structurally identical OpenAI function-tool literals (DELEGATE_TOOL … CLEAR_ALL_ACTION_LOGS_TOOL), each repeating `type:'function'`, `parameters:{type:'object', properties:{…}, required:[…]}`. Eight of them take the same single `agent_name` parameter. The required array duplicates the property keys by hand. The actual information content — name, description, params — is buried under 10 lines of envelope per tool.
impact: realtime.ts 271 → ~150 lines; every tool reads as one expression; adding a voice tool is 1-3 lines instead of 12
guidance: Two corrections/notes: (1) There are TWO identical test files importing VOICE_TOOLS, not one — api/src/services/__tests__/realtimeRoutes.test.ts AND api/tests/realtime.test.ts. Both assert reference identity and both remain green; just be aware when running gates. (2) Type the builder's props parameter permissively — e.g. Record<string, { type: string; description: string; [k: string]: unknown }> — because OpenAI function-tool properties may later need enum/items/nested-schema fields; the sketch's strict { type; description } shape would force rework. (3) Preserve key insertion order as in the sketch (type, name, description, parameters; and type, properties, required) to keep JSON.stringify output byte-identical. (4) rollback's `count` property is type 'integer' (realtime.ts:123) — covered by the string-typed `type` field, just don't hardcode 'string'.

title: requireBoardAccess(level) middleware to collapse 14 copies of the checkBoardAccess preamble in boards.ts
description: Every handler in boards.ts opens with the same two lines: `const access = await checkBoardAccess(req.params.id, req.user.userId, req.user.role, <level>); if (!access.ok) return res.status(access.status).json({ error: access.error });` — at boards.ts:102-103, 129-130, 143-144, 161-162, 175-176, 189-190, 208-209, 226-227, 239-240, 259-260, 271-272, 310-311, 332-333, 350-351. The interesting part of each handler (what it actually does to the board) starts 3-4 lines in, and the access level is an easy-to-miss string argument in the middle of the call.
impact: ~30 lines removed in boards.ts (+~14 more in projects.ts if adopted); the permission level moves to the route declaration where reviewers look for it
guidance: (1) Do NOT add a new requireBoardAccess — authorizeBoardAccess at api/src/middleware/auth.ts:835-851 already implements exactly this (introduced in security commit 24ae2135, currently dead with 0 call sites). Wire it into the routes instead; writing the sketch's function verbatim would create a duplicate of an existing helper. (2) ORDERING TRAP: the sketch puts the access middleware before validateBody, but today validation runs first (route middleware) and the access check runs inside the handler. To keep responses identical for invalid-body+no-access requests (400-validation today, would become 403/404), order as `validateBody(schema), authorizeBoardAccess(level)` on the 8 routes that have both: boards.ts PUT /:id (127), PUT /:id/workflow (141), PUT /:id/plugins (173), POST /:id/plugins/assign (187), POST /:id/plugins/remove (206), PUT /:id/mcp-auth (224), POST /:id/shares (269), PUT /:id/shares/:userId (308). (3) Known minor wire difference on the 500 path: if checkBoardAccess throws, inline code returns { error: err.message } via the handler's catch, while the middleware returns { error: 'Authorization check failed' } (auth.ts:848). No test pins this (there are no HTTP-level board-route tests; authorization.test.ts tests checkBoardAccess directly), but flag it in the commit message. (4) In converted handlers replace access.board/permission/isOwner with req.boardAccess.* — the middleware attaches no `ok` field. (5) Scope: leave agents.ts:110/122 and tasks.ts:34/60 alone (board id comes from agent/task, not req.params); leave projects.ts:160-176 (dual project+board check) and resolveBoardGitHubAuth (projects.ts:336+, query-param flow fused with token resolution) alone. projects.ts:187, 197, 227, 285 adopt authorizeBoardAccess('read', 'boardId') cleanly.

title: Extract the cache-lookup / fetch / stale-fallback pattern shared by the 5 GitHub explorer endpoints in projects.ts
description: github-activity (projects.ts:365-415), github-branches (:417-438), github-tree (:440-467), github-file (:469-518) and code-graph (:525-602) all repeat the same skeleton: resolveBoardGitHubAuth → build cacheKey → `if (cached && Date.now()-cached.time < TTL) return res.json(cached.data)` → try { fetch + map + cacheSet + res.json } catch { console.error; `if (cached) return res.json(cached.data)`; 500 }. The serve-stale-on-error policy and the cacheSet bound parameters are restated five times with slightly different max-entries magic numbers (500/500/100/1000/50) and cache-key prefixes ('', 'branches:', 'tree:', 'file:', 'cg:') that exist only because the caches would otherwise collide if merged.
impact: ~50 lines removed from a 605-line file; the 'serve stale when GitHub is down' behavior — currently discoverable only by reading 5 catch blocks — is stated once
guidance: 1) SCOPE CORRECTION: apply the helper to the 4 GET endpoints only; exclude POST /code-graph/:owner/:repo (:525-602). Its catch block (:598-601) has no stale fallback and returns `Code graph analysis failed: ${err.message}` (err detail in body); its tree-fetch failure path responds 502 inside the try (:546-550). Forcing it into serveCached would add a stale fallback that doesn't exist today, lose err.message, and break on the early 502 (res.json after headers sent / cacheSet(undefined)). 2) The single `label` param can't reproduce current logs: console messages carry per-endpoint context (e.g. `Failed to fetch tree for ${owner}/${repo}@${ref}` :463, `Failed to fetch file ${filePath} for ...` :514) while response bodies use different generic strings (`Failed to fetch file tree` :465, `Failed to fetch file content` :516). Pass two strings (logContext + responseError) to preserve both exactly. 3) Keep capturing `cached` before build() runs (the sketch already does) — current code serves the pre-fetch snapshot as stale fallback even if a concurrent request repopulated the cache mid-await. 4) Do NOT merge the five Map caches; the description's claim that key prefixes exist to prevent collisions is wrong (they are separate Maps, :21-25). One prefix is load-bearing: _fileCache is shared by github-file (`file:` keys, shaped objects) and code-graph's fetchFile (`cg-file:` keys, raw strings, :557-577) — leave untouched. 5) github-file's `if (!filePath) return 400` (:475) and github-activity's forceRefresh parsing (:372) stay outside the helper, passed as key/force. 6) No API tests cover these routes (grep over api/ matches only projects.ts), so verification is tsc + manual; keep the transformation strictly mechanical. Expected savings ~30-35 lines, not ~50.

title: Merge internalClaudeTokens.ts and internalCodexTokens.ts into one token-store route factory
description: Two routers with the same three endpoints (GET/POST/DELETE /:ownerId) against the same oauth_tokens store, including a copy-pasted 16-line 'read back and tryDecrypt to verify durable persistence' block with its load-bearing comment duplicated (internalClaudeTokens.ts:55-75 vs internalCodexTokens.ts:21-39 — same SQL, same logic, same rationale). Only the provider constant ('claude_code' vs 'codex'), the GET response shape (refreshToken vs meta), and the POST body parsing (expiresIn→expiresAt math vs meta passthrough) differ. internalCodexTokens.ts is also written in a cramped multiple-statements-per-line style unlike every sibling file.
impact: 137 lines across 2 files → ~85 in one file; the rotate-token-must-verify-persistence invariant (critical for Claude refresh-token rotation) is implemented and commented once
guidance: Sketch corrections and traps: (1) Type the factory's provider param as OAuthProvider (the union exported from services/database/oauthTokens.ts:26), not string — fetchOAuthTokenWithDbFallback and OAuthTokenRecord.provider require it, so `provider: string` fails tsc. (2) Preserve the claude POST expiry precedence exactly: expiresAt (number, epoch ms) wins over expiresIn (seconds-from-now math), internalClaudeTokens.ts:39-44; keep this inside the claude parse hook, do not invent a shared expiry parser. (3) Preserve claude's refreshToken filter `typeof refreshToken === 'string' && refreshToken ? refreshToken : null` (line 51) and codex's meta filter `meta && typeof meta === 'object' ? meta : undefined` (line 20) verbatim in their parse hooks — storeOAuthToken normalizes null/undefined identically downstream, but keeping them verbatim makes review trivial. (4) verifyPersisted must keep the no-pool semantics: getPool() returning null means DB-less deployment → treated as persisted (route returns 200), and the route 500s only when a pool exists and the read-back/decrypt mismatch occurs; keep the catch→persisted=false inside the helper. (5) Keep the merged comment with the claude version's fuller rationale (refresh tokens rotate; a lost rotated token is unrecoverable after restart) — that is the most valuable sentence in either file. (6) The order ownerId-guard → parse(400) → storeOAuthToken → verify(500) → {ok:true} must be unchanged; the Python consumers in runner-service/src/backends/{claude,codex}_token_store.py depend on these status codes and GET body field names (claude: accessToken/refreshToken/expiresAt; codex: accessToken/expiresAt/meta). (7) No JS tests cover these routes, so the only safety net is tsc — diff the two serialize/parse hooks against the original handlers line by line. Minor: specs/api/swarm-and-internal.md cites internalClaudeTokens.ts by filename, but docs are out of scope per the rules.

title: Delete six dead has*CredentialsForAgent/Board exports
description: hasWordPressCredentialsForAgent / hasWordPressCredentialsForBoard (wordpress.ts:39-47), hasJiraCredentialsForAgent / hasJiraCredentialsForBoard (jira.ts:19-27), and hasS3CredentialsForAgent / hasS3CredentialsForBoard (s3.ts:14-22) are exported but imported nowhere in the repo (verified by grep over api/, frontend/, runner-service/ excluding node_modules — only the definitions match). The get*CredentialsForAgent siblings ARE used (services/jiraMcp.ts:4, services/s3Mcp.ts:17, services/wordpressMcp.ts:6); these boolean variants are vestigial scaffolding that misleads readers into thinking some caller gates on them.
sketch: Remove the six functions (and the now-unused hasOAuthToken import in each file if nothing else uses it). No call sites to update. If the connector-factory proposal is implemented, these die as part of it.
impact: ~30 lines removed; eliminates a false signal that connection state is queried somewhere outside the /status endpoints
guidance: Sketch is correct as written; two refinements. (a) The conditional "remove the now-unused hasOAuthToken import if nothing else uses it" is definite, not conditional: in wordpress.ts, jira.ts, and s3.ts, hasOAuthToken appears only inside the six dead functions — remove it from the import list in all three files or tsc/noUnusedLocals may flag it. (b) The proposal undercounts the dead family: the identical zero-call-site pattern also exists in five more route files — hasGdriveTokensForAgent/Board (gdrive.ts:32-40), hasGmailTokensForAgent/Board (gmail.ts:31-39), hasOutlookTokensForAgent/Board (outlook.ts:33-41), hasGitHubTokensForAgent/Board (github.ts:128-136), hasSlackTokensForAgent/Board (slack.ts:90-98) — verified by grep to have only definition matches. If extending, note the import-cleanup trap differs there: gmail.ts, gdrive.ts, outlook.ts (and onedrive.ts) ALSO call hasOAuthToken directly in their /status handlers (gmail.ts:103, gdrive.ts:104, outlook.ts:105, onedrive.ts:103), so keep the hasOAuthToken import in those files; in github.ts and slack.ts it is only used by the dead functions and can be removed. Gate to re-run after: api tsc + 244 tests.

title: Split the 200-line PUT /tasks/:id handler into named phases and share the board-move core with bulk-move
description: PUT /tasks/:id (tasks.ts:198-399) is a single handler doing seven things in sequence: stop-running-executor (:216-226), board-move with column revalidation (:238-258), column-only validation (:259-276), scalar field diffing into editedFields (:279-298), history-entry assembly (:300-322), a second field-by-field re-application of the same patch onto the in-memory task (:324-359), then persistence, auto-refine, three emits, and the audit log (:361-393). The memTask sync block restates every assignment from :279-286 a second time, so adding one task field requires touching four places in this one handler. Meanwhile POST /bulk-move (:402-495) reimplements the board-move + history + clear-state + emit pipeline per task with small drift (e.g. bulk history entries get `bulk: true`, fields hardcoded).
sketch:
Keep the route, extract phase functions (composing with the clearExecutionState/stopTaskExecutor helpers from the other proposal):

  async function resolveBoardMove(task, { boardId, column }, user): Promise<{ ok: boolean; status?; error?; boardChanged; statusChanged; oldBoardId; oldBoardName; newBoardName }>
  function applyTaskEdits(target, patch): string[]            // returns editedFields; called once for the copy AND once for memTask — kills the duplicated assignment block at :324-359
  function buildHistoryEntry({ now, by, boardMove, statusChange, editedFields, bulk })  // shared with bulk-move (:440-447)
  function emitTaskUpdate(mgr, task)                           // task:updated + agent:updated pair, used at :382-386, :474-478, :527

PUT /:id body becomes ~40 lines of phase calls reading like the comment headers it already has; bulk-move's per-task loop becomes resolveBoardMove + applyTaskEdits + buildHistoryEntry + clearExecutionState + emitTaskUpdate.
impact: tasks.ts ~945 → ~820 lines; the copy/memTask dual-write becomes one function applied twice; bulk-move and single-move can no longer drift apart
guidance: Descope and fix the sketch before implementing. (1) DROP the resolveBoardMove/buildHistoryEntry sharing with bulk-move; the paths differ on purpose: PUT's column-only change returns a strict 400 on invalid column (:267-272) while bulk silently falls back via validateColumn (:412-414); PUT validates destination access once per request, bulk once per request too — a per-task resolveBoardMove would re-run checkBoardAccess+getBoardById per task, changing query volume and failure surface; PUT pushes history with fields/from only when statusChanged (:317-320), bulk hardcodes fields ['boardId','status']+from unconditionally even when the column didn't change (:441-447) — history is persisted JSON; PUT orders memTask-sync → save → auto-refine, bulk orders save → memTask-sync → auto-refine. (2) setTaskSignal placement trap: PUT calls it INSIDE if(memTask) (:356) so DB-only tasks are never signaled; bulk calls it OUTSIDE (:468). A shared helper must not unify this. (3) bulk's memTask sync is gated on oldStatus !== targetColumn (:452), so a same-column cross-board bulk move never updates memTask.boardId — latent bug; a shared sync would silently fix it (behavior change, flag separately instead). (4) The two clear-execution-state blocks are NOT duplicates: the memTask one (:345-357) additionally deletes _pendingOnEnter/_completedActionIdx and sets completedActionIdx=null; applying that to the copy would persist completedActionIdx=null via saveTaskDirectly(task) where the current DB write leaves it untouched — keep two variants or parameterize. (5) Sketch error: emitTaskUpdate must NOT absorb :527 — /:id/stop emits only task:updated with a constructed { ...target, agentId } payload and no agent:updated; the helper fits exactly :382-386 and :474-478. (6) applyTaskEdits must exclude column/boardId: memTask :331-332 carries the RESOLVED status from validateColumn fallback (:252-254), not the raw patch column; keep status/boardId sync as a separate line after board resolution. With this scoping the effort drops from large to medium and risk from medium to low.

title: Move the 77-line agent zod schemas out of agents.ts into schemas/agents.ts, and fold the twin access guards into a factory
description: Sibling routes already keep zod schemas in api/src/schemas/ (tasks.ts imports from ../schemas/tasks.js at :7-11, boards.ts from ../schemas/boards.js, projects.ts from ../schemas/projects.js), but agents.ts inlines createAgentSchema/updateAgentSchema at agents.ts:12-88, pushing the first route definition to line 128 of a 726-line file. Separately, requireAgentAccess (agents.ts:104-113) and requireAgentEditAccess (:117-125) are identical except for the 'read'/'edit' literal — the comment at :117-118 is the only thing distinguishing them. Also note the routes at agents.ts:691-724 are dedented two levels relative to every other route in the factory, visually suggesting they sit outside the router when they do not.
impact: agents.ts 726 → ~630 lines and starts with routes instead of a schema wall; one access-guard implementation instead of two near-twins; matches the established schemas/ convention
guidance: Three traps to respect, all easy: (1) Keep `import { z } from 'zod'` in agents.ts — `err instanceof z.ZodError` at :212 and :234 still needs it after the schema move. (2) Verbatim move ONLY: do not also adopt `validateBody` from lib/validate.js the way tasks.ts does — agents.ts's inline parse + catch produces its own error-response shape, and switching would change the wire format for validation failures. Export both schemas from the new schemas/agents.ts (updateAgentSchema depends on createAgentSchema via .partial().extend(), so they must move together). (3) The guards close over `agentManager`, so the `agentAccess(level)` factory must be defined inside agentRoutes(agentManager), at the same spot the current functions occupy (above the first use at :181). Preserve the two explanatory comments — the 'Agents without a board are accessible to everyone (legacy)' note at :108 and the read-only-shares rationale at :115-116 — by attaching them to the factory and/or the two derived consts. Minor: `const parsed: any` at :200 should stay as-is.

title: Flatten the PATCH /agents/:id/tasks/:taskId update-dispatch chain
description: agents.ts:452-535 mixes three styles in one handler: (a) the try{ at :453 is followed by a body that is NOT indented (lines 454-521 sit at the same level as the try), so the catch at :522 visually floats; (b) recurrence/taskType/isManual updates are applied imperatively up front (:474-488) while text/repo/storage/status are dispatched through a 30-line else-if precedence chain (:490-519) whose three trailing branches (`else if (recurrence !== undefined) … else if (taskType !== undefined) … else if (isManual !== undefined)`) exist only to re-find the task for the response; (c) if NO recognized field is present the final else silently calls `toggleTask()` (:518) — a surprising hidden behavior that nothing in the route name or comments advertises.
impact: ~85-line handler → ~50 lines with one obvious flow; the hidden toggleTask fallback becomes an explicit, commented line; the broken indentation stops misrepresenting scope
guidance: Corrections to the sketch: (1) The reviewer missed that `title` (agents.ts:491) is NOT in the :494 else-if chain — a {title}-only body (sent by frontend TaskDetailModal.tsx:111-113 when only the title changed) today runs updateTaskTitle AND THEN toggleTask via the final else, flipping the task's status. Including title in the `touched` array changes this behavior. This is almost certainly a latent bug worth fixing, but it must be flagged to the owner as an explicit behavior change bundled with the refactor, not done silently. (2) Compute `touched` with the SAME predicates as the dispatch branches: status must be truthiness-checked (`else if (status)` today), not `!== undefined`, or {status:null}/{status:''} bodies stop reaching toggleTask — another wire change on a public route. (3) Use the hedged variant: keep text/repo/storage/status as mutually exclusive else-ifs (today {text,status} applies text and silently ignores status); only hoist the response lookup and delete the three no-op tail branches. (4) Keep the `oldTask` guard on the isManual block (:484-488) — it dereferences oldTask directly; the recurrence/taskType guards can be dropped safely since updateTaskRecurrence/updateTaskType null-check internally. (5) Re-find-for-response is identity-safe: all mgr.updateTaskX methods (services/agentManager/tasks.ts:178,250,264,278,293,308) return the live in-memory task object. (6) The empty-body -> toggleTask path MUST survive: frontend api.ts:270-274 depends on it; add the comment the sketch proposes. (7) No route-level tests cover this handler, so nothing breaks in CI — but also no safety net; verify manually with the six frontend payload shapes ({}, {status}, {text}, {title}/{title,text}, {recurrence}, {isManual}, {taskType}, {repoFullName,repoProvider}, {storagePath,storageProvider}).

title: One shared getUserBoardIds helper instead of two private copies plus an inline rebuild
description: The 'resolve the caller's accessible board IDs, swallowing errors' helper exists twice with different return types: tasks.ts:13-20 returns Promise<string[]> while agents.ts:94-101 returns Promise<Set<string>> — same body otherwise. boards.ts:58-59 re-derives the Set inline from getBoardsByUser. The type split forces readers to check which flavor they hold (tasks.ts uses array for SQL ANY($1), agents.ts uses Set.has).
impact: ~16 duplicated lines removed; one canonical 'visible boards' resolution with one error policy instead of three
guidance: 1) EXCLUDE boards.ts:58-59 from the dedup. It is inside a try/catch that returns 500 on getBoardsByUser failure; the shared error-swallowing helper would change that failure to a 200 with empty/filtered results. Leave the 2-line inline derivation (or keep calling getBoardsByUser directly there). The dedup is tasks.ts + agents.ts only. 2) Do NOT add the helper to api/src/services/database.ts. Six tests mock the whole module via mock.module('../database.js', { namedExports }) and projectTenantIsolation.test.ts imports routes/tasks.js under that mock — a new export would be undefined and throw at runtime. Put the helper in a new small module (e.g. api/src/lib/boardAccess.ts) that imports getBoardsByUser from services/database.js; mock.module intercepts the resolved database module for the helper too, so existing test mocks keep working unchanged. 3) tasks.ts adaptations beyond the sketched spread: lines 97 and 790 use boardIds.length === 0 and must become .size === 0 (tsc catches a miss); lines 698-699 push boardIds into both summaryParams and dailyParams — spread to [...boardIds] in both. 4) Minor: agents.ts has 12 call sites (129, 139, 152, 159, 165, 361, 375, 565, 693, 701, 709, 718), not 8.

title: Share the agent-status handler logic duplicated between leaderTools.ts and agents.ts
description: Four endpoints exist twice with the same shape: /all-statuses with its project filter (leaderTools.ts:57-70) vs /statuses (agents.ts:137-148 — identical lowercase-project filter copy-pasted), /swarm-status (leaderTools.ts:73-75 vs agents.ts:164-167), /by-project/:project (leaderTools.ts:78-81 vs agents.ts:151-155), /project-summary (leaderTools.ts:84-86 vs agents.ts:158-161). The only real difference is that the agents.ts versions resolve and pass userBoardIds while the leaderTools versions call the same agentManager methods without the third argument — a scoping difference that is invisible unless you diff the files, and that may or may not be intentional.
impact: ~40 duplicated lines collapse to ~25 shared; the leader-vs-user scoping difference becomes explicit; the duplicated project-filter snippet exists once
guidance: 1) The scoping difference IS intentional, not an accident: getters.ts:31 ("Fallback: no board info means show all (admin-like)") plus docs/API_REFERENCE.md documenting /api/leader-tools as swarm-leader endpoints. Frame the refactor as documenting this, and write a comment at the leaderTools mount saying the unscoped variant is deliberate (leader sees the whole swarm). 2) Express is 4.21 (api/package.json:22): the sketch makes every handler async, but leaderTools handlers are currently synchronous — a sync throw there reaches Express's error middleware today, while a rejection inside an async handler in Express 4 does not. For strict preservation, have the factory return a plain sync handler when scoped=false (only the scoped variant needs await). 3) getUserBoardIds (agents.ts:94-101) is closure-local and used by ~10 other routes in agents.ts (129, 152, 159, 165, 361, 375, 565, 693, 701, 709, 718); move it to the shared module once and import it back into agents.ts, preserving its catch-returns-empty-Set semantics — do not fork a second copy named getUserBoardIdSet as the sketch implies. 4) The unscoped variant must not call getUserBoardIds at all — passing a real Set would board-scope leader-tools and break swarm-wide visibility (getters.ts:30). 5) Keep route registration order in agents.ts unchanged: /statuses, /by-project/:project, /project-summary, /swarm-status are registered before /:id/status (line 181) and /:id (line 188); extract handlers only, keep mounts in place. 6) Path names differ (/statuses vs /all-statuses) — keep them at the mount points; do not unify URLs. 7) No tests cover any of these eight routes, so smoke-test both route families manually after the change (one scoped user with limited boards, one leader call).
